strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Elitez Vantage owns the bridge between consulting-grade methodology and SaaS-grade pricing — defended by SG-licensed accountability that AI-native subs cannot replicate.

dimensions.label: Buyer-owned JSON vs vendor SaaS hostage

attack_plans.icp: SG-incorporated SME, USD 5-30M revenue, founder-led, considering pivot/fundraise/board upgrade in next 12 months
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: 12-18k SG SMEs in revenue band × 25% annual buy rate × USD 5,000 avg ticket = USD 19M; PSG halves effective price and ~doubles capture rate.
attack_plans.why_gap: AI-native subs (ChatGPT Pro, Claude Max, Manus) are not on the IMDA pre-approved list. SG boutiques (Asia Insight, Kadence) charge SGD 8-20k and don't shape the deliverable for PSG submission. This is a structural 50% pricing arbitrage AI subs cannot close without becoming a SG Pte Ltd.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Singapore Pte Ltd accountability + IMDA pre-approval submission + 5-day delivery + editorial PDF that reads as human-curated, not AI-generated. Effective customer price SGD 750 after PSG.

gtm.channel: ASME, SBF, SGTECH founder communities; SG accelerator alumni networks; PSG advisor partner referrals
gtm.pitch: Get a McKinsey-shape competitor deck at the price of a single ChatGPT Pro annual seat — and PSG covers half.
gtm.pricing: Starter SGD 1,500 / SGD 750 post-PSG; quarterly refresh at SGD 4,500/yr (SGD 2,250 post-PSG)
gtm.content: Case studies: 'SG founder retired ChatGPT for board CI'; PSG submission walkthrough; 60-second video demo of agent pipeline

attack_plans.icp: HKMA-licensed FI / fintech sandbox graduate; mid-market USD 30-100M revenue; needs documented third-party CI for HKMA submissions
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: 9-12k HK mid-market firms × 30% annual buy rate × USD 8,000 avg ticket = USD 25M; sandbox cohort and FI subset addressable concentrated within ~600 firms.
attack_plans.why_gap: Bain/PwC are USD 250k+ and 8 weeks out; AlphaSense doesn't shape outputs for HKMA submissions; AI-native subs fail on accountability requirement; F&S APAC doesn't currently package an HKMA-fintech-specific compliance pack.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Singapore Pte Ltd accountability, HK-incorporated entity by Y2, methodology shaped to HKMA supervisory-sandbox post-exit submission requirements; SEA-source weighting matches Greater Bay competitive landscape.

gtm.channel: HK fintech incubators (Cyberport, HKSTP), HKMA sandbox alumni, Big-4 HK fintech advisors as referral partners
gtm.pitch: The HKMA-submission-ready competitor pack, delivered in 10 days, signed by a Singapore-licensed analyst.
gtm.pricing: Enterprise tier SGD 12,000; quarterly refresh × 4 included Y1; HKMA-overlay add-on SGD 3,000
gtm.content: HKMA supervisory-sandbox submission template (gated); whitepaper on fintech competitor coverage requirements; two-pager case from one anchored sandbox graduate

attack_plans.niche_name: Methodology license for inhouse strategy teams
attack_plans.icp: Series B-C Singapore/HK startup with 1-3 in-house strategy/BizOps analysts; reports to founder/CEO; needs reproducible CI process
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~400 SG/HK Series B-C startups × 20% who'd license a methodology in any 12-month window × USD 18k avg license = USD 1.4M/yr; 5-yr cumulative ~USD 8M assuming sustained category growth.
attack_plans.why_gap: OSS templates (CrewAI, LangGraph, Anthropic SDK) are scattered with no canonical FIELD-DICTIONARY + agent specs + quality gates. Boutique consultancies (Asia Insight, Kadence) keep their methodology private. SaaS vendors (Crayon, Klue, AlphaSense) hold buyer data hostage.
attack_plans.why_we_win: We're the only player shipping a 600+ line FIELD-DICTIONARY, 9 numbered agent specs, and inter-agent quality gates as the product. The buyer keeps both the methodology and the JSON outputs forever; methodology survives team turnover.

gtm.channel: Strategy/BizOps director networks (Wharton APAC, INSEAD SG, IDEO Slack); founder + COO networks; selective open-source teasers on GitHub to seed inbound
gtm.pitch: Stop renting your CI process. Buy the methodology, run it forever — your team owns the rerun.
gtm.pricing: Annual Partner SGD 50,000 (covers methodology license + Y1 quarterly delivery + 4 strategy calls)
gtm.content: Open-source the JSON schema; methodology docs gated behind license; quarterly 'CI methodology' substack to position founder as category-thinker

attack_plans.icp: MAS-licensed bank, capital-markets firm, asset manager; needs Notice-656 / TRMG-compliant third-party CI signed by named SG-licensed analyst
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~600 MAS-licensed FIs × 30% annual ad-hoc buy × USD 30,000 avg ticket = USD 5.4M; high concentration unlocks faster than dispersed SG SME segment.
attack_plans.why_gap: MAS Notice 656/TRMG accountability requirements + 2025 deepfake advisory squeezed AI-native subs out of FI buying entirely. F&S Singapore is procurement-acceptable but slow; Asia Insight does fieldwork but doesn't position as compliance-shaped CI.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Pte Ltd named analyst on every deliverable; MAS-aligned methodology appendix and source-residency disclosure; editorial PDF and JSON reproducibility match MAS audit posture.

gtm.channel: MAS regulatory community events; ABS (Association of Banks Singapore) procurement leads; Big-4 SG financial-services partners as referrers
gtm.pitch: TRMG-friendly competitor intelligence — signed by a named SG-licensed analyst, source-residency documented, JSON-reproducible.
gtm.pricing: Custom Enterprise SGD 18,000-30,000 per engagement; annual partnership SGD 80,000+
gtm.content: MAS Notice 656 vendor-checklist whitepaper (gated); case study from one anchored FI; founder by-line in Banking Asia or similar

attack_plans.icp: Founder/board member buying single-decision CI artefact (fundraise / pivot / acquisition diligence); 5-7 day timeline; needs board-pass aesthetic
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Estimated ~5,000 one-shot CI buys/yr in SG+HK at ~USD 5,000-8,000 avg ticket × ~15-20% addressable = ~USD 7M/yr.
attack_plans.why_gap: F&S off-the-shelf reports are USD 3.5-10k but generic; F&S custom is USD 15-25k and slow; ChatGPT Pro produces fast output that fails board read; Asia Insight is 4-6 weeks. The 5-7-day editorial-board-ready pocket is empty.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Editorial PDF designed to read as human-curated (Playfair + Inter, slate/amber palette); one-shot pricing aligned with F&S off-the-shelf bracket; 5-7 day delivery beats every boutique competitor on speed without sacrificing aesthetic.

gtm.channel: VC partner referrals (deal-prep CI for portfolio cos), corp-dev advisors, M&A intermediaries — people who buy CI repeatedly for one-shot decisions
gtm.pitch: F&S aesthetic, 5-day delivery, methodology you can audit. The pre-board-meeting CI artefact your CFO won't push back on.
gtm.pricing: Standard SGD 4,500 with named-analyst signature and 30-min board-briefing call
gtm.content: Side-by-side comparison page (us vs F&S off-the-shelf vs Asia Insight); 'how to read a CI deck in a board meeting' guide; example sanitised PDF
